explanations.4: The dind suffix stands for 'Docker in Docker' — it's a special Docker image that lets you run Docker commands inside a Docker container.
explanations.8: This YAML syntax means the .dagger section inherits all settings from .docker, so it gets the Docker service and variables automatically.
explanations.11: This line downloads and installs Dagger (a tool for defining pipelines) by piping a shell script directly into sh — basically running the installer script.
explanations.15: The dagger call command is looking for code in the src folder and using .config as its configuration, then running something named 'run-all'.
